message:
feat(robot_sdk_contracts): Cx-first RustSdkTransport::call (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

Add Cx-first siblings to `RustSdkTransport`'s two public async
methods. Routes IPC RPC through tick-78's `call_rpc_with_cx`,
which threads cx through all 4 await points in the socket
round-trip (connect, write, flush, read).

Additions (strictly additive, asupersync-runtime-gated):

  * `RustSdkTransport::call_with_cx<T>(cx, command, payload)` —
    Cx-first parallel of `call`. Uses
    `IpcClient::call_rpc_with_cx` instead of `call_rpc`.
    Payload shape + response decoding are unchanged — only the
    IPC transport layer becomes cx-aware.

  * `RustSdkTransport::call_value_with_cx(cx, command, payload)`
    — pure delegate to `call_with_cx<serde_json::Value>`,
    mirroring the legacy `call_value → call` delegation.

Regression test (new)
`call_with_cx_surfaces_watcher_not_running`:

  * Builds a `RustSdkTransport` with a guaranteed-nonexistent
    socket path.
  * Calls `call_value_with_cx(&cx, "get-text", json!({"pane_id":
    1}))`.
  * Asserts the error is
    `RustSdkTransportError::Transport(UserVarError::WatcherNotRunning
    { .. })` — proves the Cx-first call reaches the socket
    check inside `send_request_with_id_with_cx`.

Full round-trip testing is out of scope for unit tests (it
requires a running watcher daemon). The existing tests cover
spec shapes; this adds error-path cx-path coverage to the
transport layer.

Verification (local fork bypass):
  * robot_sdk_contracts:: — 41 passed / 0 failed (tick 86: 40
    -> tick 87: 41, +1 new cx-path error test)
  * cargo build -p frankenterm-core --lib — clean (50.19s)
  * cargo fmt -p frankenterm-core -- --check — clean

Scanned other low-cx 2-pub-async candidates:
- distributed.rs: both `.get`/.post` ALREADY take `&Cx` — no
  migration needed (the distributed feature was written
  Cx-first from the start).
- Cx-first robot_sdk_contracts.rs trail COMPLETE — both public
  async methods have cx siblings.

Bead: ft-xbnl0.2.3

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
